latestPlannerResponse.message: I've updated my understanding, scope, and acceptance criteria based on our discussion.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: On the Brand Vault screen (/vault), render a grid of four category tiles—Logos, Fonts, Guidelines, Visuals. Each tile shows a category label, an icon, and counts derived from BrandAsset records: a required org-wide active-asset count, and, when a current project context exists, a secondary per-project active-asset count. Counts are based on distinct BrandAsset rows (not versions), include only status=active, and exclude archived. Tiles are read-only and non-clickable in this story; listing and filtering are handled by separate planned stories.

in: Render four tiles (Logos, Fonts, Guidelines, Visuals) on Brand Vault (/vault)
Show category label and icon per tile
Show org-wide active BrandAsset count per tile
Show current-project active BrandAsset count per tile when a project context exists; otherwise omit the project count
Compute counts on BrandAsset (not BrandAssetVersion) with status=active; exclude archived
Accessible labels including category and counts
Backend API to provide counts for the current org and optional current project
out: Clickable tiles or deep links to lists (handled by the planned list story)
Any filtering UI or project selector (handled by the planned filter story)
Uploading/editing assets, search, or inline previews
Admin/category management or changing category taxonomy
Displaying categories beyond the four specified

assumptions: Category-to-assetType mapping: Logos=logo, Fonts=font, Guidelines=guideline, Visuals=image+footage.
Assets with asset_type not in the mapping (e.g., audio, other) are not represented by tiles in this story.
Counts include only BrandAsset rows where status=active; archived are excluded.
Org context (orgId) is available from authenticated session/app state; optional project context (projectId) may be present from global state or route.
Authorization rules are enforced server-side so counts reflect only assets the requester can access.
Icons will be proposed and mapped in the UI; placeholders are acceptable initially.
acceptanceCriteria: On /vault, exactly four tiles appear labeled Logos, Fonts, Guidelines, and Visuals; tiles render even when counts are zero.
Each tile displays an icon aligned with the category (placeholder acceptable until final set is approved).
Org-wide count per tile equals the number of distinct BrandAsset records with the mapped asset types and status=active for the current organization; BrandAssetVersion records are not counted.
If a current project context exists, a secondary per-project count is shown per tile for that project’s active assets; if no project context exists, only org-wide counts are shown.
Tiles are non-clickable and do not navigate.
While counts load, a visible loading state is shown; on failure, a non-blocking error state appears and the page remains usable.
Each tile has an accessible name that includes the category and presented counts (e.g., via aria-label), and counts are announced to screen readers.

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for View category tiles with icons and counts

increments.title: UI tiles on /vault with backend counts endpoint
increments.intent:
- Backend (Rivvy Portal Services / Portal API)
  - Add REST endpoint to Portal API (ifc-mm2fbrdg-s9zlg): GET /api/v1/brand-assets/category-counts
    - Purpose: return counts of active BrandAsset rows grouped by asset_type for a given org, and optionally for a given project within that org.
    - AuthZ: Require authenticated user; validate the requested orgId is one the user belongs to. If projectId provided, ensure it belongs to orgId. Enforce standard access checks so counts reflect only assets the user can access.
    - Request: query params orgId (required, UUID), projectId (optional, UUID).
    - Response JSON:
      { "org": { "logo": number, "font": number, "guideline": number, "image": number, "footage": number }, "project": { "logo": number, "font": number, "guideline": number, "image": number, "footage": number } | null }
      - Only includes status=active assets; excludes archived.
      - For org counts: include all assets in org where status=active (visibility rules enforced by authz/policies).
      - For project counts: only assets with project_id = projectId and status=active.
    - Service/repository logic (illustrative):
      - Org counts:
        SELECT asset_type, COUNT(*) FROM brand_asset WHERE org_id = :orgId AND status = 'active' GROUP BY asset_type;
      - Project counts (if projectId):
        SELECT asset_type, COUNT(*) FROM brand_asset WHERE org_id = :orgId AND project_id = :projectId AND status = 'active' GROUP BY asset_type;
      - Merge into response with default zeros for missing types.
    - Errors: 400 for invalid/missing orgId; 403 if user not authorized for org/project; 200 with zeros if valid but no assets.
    - Tests: unit tests for service (status filter, org vs project), integration tests for controller happy path and 403.

- Frontend (Rivvy Portal UI / /vault)
  - API client: add fetchBrandAssetCounts(orgId: string, projectId?: string) -> Promise<{ org: Record<string, number>; project?: Record<string, number> }>.
  - Category mapping and icons proposal:
    - const CATEGORY_CONFIG = [
      { key: 'logos', label: 'Logos', assetTypes: ['logo'], icon: 'icon-logo' },
      { key: 'fonts', label: 'Fonts', assetTypes: ['font'], icon: 'icon-font' },
      { key: 'guidelines', label: 'Guidelines', assetTypes: ['guideline'], icon: 'icon-guideline' },
      { key: 'visuals', label: 'Visuals', assetTypes: ['image', 'footage'], icon: 'icon-visuals' }
    ];
    - Map icons to design-system equivalents: logo=image-badge, font=typography, guideline=book/rule, visuals=gallery/photo-stack.
  - UI component: CategoryTilesGrid
    - Props: orgCounts, projectCounts (optional), loading, error.
    - Renders a responsive 2x2 grid of the four tiles in the given order.
    - For each tile: sum counts from the API buckets matching assetTypes (e.g., Visuals sums image+footage) for org and project (if present).
    - Visual layout: prominent org count; if project present, show a secondary “This project: X”.
    - Accessibility: aria-label per tile like “Logos, organization count 12, this project 3” (omit project phrase if absent); counts area marked aria-live=polite.
    - Loading: show four skeleton tiles. Error: inline non-blocking alert with retry; tiles hidden during hard error or shown with zeros if partial acceptable.
    - Tiles are non-clickable (no onClick/role/hover affordances).
  - Brand Vault (/vault) integration:
    - Read orgId from session/app context; read optional projectId from global selection or route param (do not implement selector here).
    - On mount, call fetchBrandAssetCounts(orgId, projectId?) and pass to CategoryTilesGrid.
    - Handle empty results by showing zeros and maintaining layout.

- Non-functional
  - DB indexes: ensure (org_id, status, asset_type) and (org_id, project_id, status, asset_type) to keep queries fast.
  - Logging/metrics: log endpoint usage/latency (no PII).
  - i18n: wrap labels (Logos, Fonts, Guidelines, Visuals, This project) in translation keys.
  - Optional feature flag to gate UI during rollout.

- Representative test cases
  - Backend: zeros when no assets; excludes archived; correct org vs project counts; 403 when user lacks access; 200 with only org bucket when no projectId.
  - Frontend: tiles show zero counts; project sub-count appears only when provided; aria-labels include numbers; skeleton during loading; error banner on fetch failure; Visuals correctly aggregates image+footage.

answers.1a591ae0-1c18-4469-aa7d-7b4bd8e1576e: The next story makes them clickable
answers.e8e49a71-d96b-4a1c-b4f6-a49354353dbb: Have counts for org wide and per project counts

messages.content: Welcome! We’re ready to work on “View category tiles with icons and counts.” I have the product backlog and full architecture context available. Would you like to highlight any specific architecture entities or diagrams (e.g., UI screens, services, data entities) as especially relevant for this story?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here’s my current understanding of the category tiles feature on Brand Vault. I have 7 questions about categories, counting rules, scope, icons, and zero-count display.

Answers to your questions:

Q: Confirm the exact category set and display labels mapped to BrandAsset.assetType.
A: Logos, Fonts, Guidelines, Visuals

Q: What is the default scope for counts before the filtering story (All vs Org-only vs specific Project)? If Project, how is it chosen?
A: Org-level or specific Project

Q: Should counts include only status=active and exclude archived, or any other visibility/status rules?
A: Yes

Q: Are tiles intentionally non-clickable for this story, or should they deep-link to the planned list view?
A: The next story makes them clickable

Q: Do you have a prescribed icon set and mapping per category, or should we propose one?
A: propose them

Q: Should categories with zero assets be shown or hidden?
A: shown

Q: Should counts include only project-scoped assets from projects the user can access, or all projects in the org regardless of membership?
A: Have counts for org wide and per project counts
I've updated my understanding, scope, and acceptance criteria based on our discussion.
Okay, let's see if we need to break this work item into parts...
This item is small enough, so let's get the software developer to review this.
